summary: Iteration 4: full end-to-end validation of SOPRA PM after MongoDB → MSSQL 2022 migration. Backend (22 pytest cases) all green; frontend integration flows verified (UserPicker → numeric actor_id, comment POST, backlog PATCH with actor_id query param, status change persisted, activity auto-log). Old Mongo test files deleted and rewritten for SQL Server.

minor.issue: Frontend sends actor_id as JSON string "1" in body instead of int; backend accepts due to Pydantic coercion but request payload is inconsistent with PATCH (?actor_id=1 numeric). Not breaking.

issues: Repeated console warnings 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' during initial mount / navigation. Cosmetic only — charts render fine after settle.

action_items: (optional) coerce actor_id to int in frontend body payload before POST /api/backlog/{id}/comments to keep the wire type consistent with the PATCH query param.
(optional) suppress Recharts width/height warnings by giving ResponsiveContainer a fixed minHeight or aspect.
critical_code_review_comments: db.py:_insert_returning_id_sync — combines INSERT and SELECT SCOPE_IDENTITY() in one execute; works with pymssql multi-recordset handling, but calling nextset() first before fetchone() would be more idiomatic. Currently OK because pymssql fetchone on the SELECT recordset returns the Id row.

server.py TeamMember PATCH endpoint quietly returns the current row if no fields were provided (fields list empty) and does NOT raise 404 even if member_id doesn't exist (it re-queries and returns 404 only then). Fine — but the empty-body PATCH is a silent no-op.
server.py TRACKED_FIELDS logs actor_id even if actor_id is None (Guest picker default) — Activity rows get NULL ActorId, which is fine per schema but produces 'unknown user' entries in UI.
csv_join('Nexora,Internal') style commas inside area names would break splitting. Areas are constrained to a known set so this is acceptable.
test_credentials: N/A — app has no auth, uses UserPicker (localStorage key `sopra_pm_actor_id`) to set actor_id for audit trail.
seed_data_creation: Tests create WB-TESTDEL / WB-TESTCASCADE / TEST_ProjDelete throwaway rows and clean them up. Only touch on the 36 seeded backlog items is one temporary status flip that is reverted (test_backlog_patch_logs_activity_and_status_done_sets_percent). No permanent mutations to production data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: MSSQL migration is stable. All 22 backend pytest cases pass in ~170s (network to sopra-db.sopra.services:51433 is the bottleneck — each pymssql connection is single-use, no pool). Frontend routes: /, /projects, /backlog, /sprint-board, /sprints, /roadmap, /team. Nav testids: nav-dashboard, nav-projects, nav-backlog, nav-board, nav-sprints, nav-roadmap, nav-team. UserPicker testid=user-picker with menuitems by member name. Edit dialog testids: form-* (fields), form-save, form-cancel, activity-panel, comment-composer, comment-input, comment-submit, activity-filter-{all,comment,change}. actor_id stored under localStorage key 'sopra_pm_actor_id' as numeric string. Backend expected constants verified: total_sp=228, done_sp=16, completion_pct=7.0, 36 backlog items, 7 projects, 4 quarters, sprint velocity rows == sprint count (LEFT JOIN).
rca of the issue: None — no bugs found. Migration is functionally complete: DDL clean, seed idempotent, IDs are int-identity, FKs enforce ON DELETE SET NULL for Projects and ON DELETE CASCADE for Activity, tracked-field diff logging works via actor_id query param, UserPicker persists numeric actor id to localStorage and dispatches actor-changed event to sync components.
